items.title: India stocks up gas from spot markets as West Asia tensions flare
items.summary: Indian refiners are increasingly turning to the spot market for cooking gas—or liquefied petroleum gas (LPG)—and liquefied natural gas (LNG) to secure supplies. More LPG is being sourced from the US, while LNG is being bought from the US, Angola, Algeria and Oman

items.title: India's June exports shine through West Asia dark clouds; trade deficit widens to five-month high
items.summary: India's goods exports increased by 15.5% in June, reaching $40.41 billion. However, the trade deficit expanded significantly to $30.43 billion due to higher imports. Oil, gold, and electronics imports drove this substantial increase in the trade deficit. Exports to West Asia showed moderate growth after earlier disruptions from regional conflicts. Free trade pacts and market diversification are boosting India's overall export competitiveness.

items.title: Aditya Birla Renewables Acquires Sprng Energy
items.summary: Aditya Birla Renewables acquired Sprng Energy's holding company for Rs 17,200 crore. This significant deal catapults the company's generation capacity to 9.2 gigawatts. Sprng Energy, previously owned by Shell Plc, operates across India. Shell had purchased Sprng Energy three years ago from Actis. The acquisition strengthens India's energy future and industrial competitiveness.

items.title: Chinese lessor moves DGCA to deregister, repossess four SpiceJet aircraft
items.summary: SpiceJet is facing action from lessors seeking to deregister four grounded Boeing 737 MAX aircraft. The airline maintains that its operations will not be affected, but legal experts caution that deregistration could impact the airline's fleet and operational capacity.

items.title: BSNL operational revenue grows 10% in June quarter
items.summary: State-run BSNL saw operational revenues grow ten percent in Q1FY27. Consumer mobility and enterprise businesses fueled this significant revenue increase. Average revenue per user for consumer mobility rose to ₹102.7. The consumer broadband segment experienced a slight decrease in revenue. These provisional figures indicate BSNL's positive business performance.

items.summary: SpaceX went public a month ago in a record IPO. Elon Musk's space and AI company was added to the Nasdaq-100 last week.

items.title: Lessors aim to reclaim SpiceJet aircraft due to increasing unpaid rentals
items.summary: Aircraft lessors are repossessing planes from SpiceJet after the airline failed to pay lease rentals. The finance leasing arm of Industrial and Commercial Bank of China requested the DGCA to take back four Boeing 737 Max aircraft. Nearly eighty percent of SpiceJet's fleet is currently grounded amid continued financial woes and employee salary issues. The airline has run out of cash despite raising funds, which were used for statutory dues.

items.title: Chipotle is opening its first restaurant in Mexico
items.summary: Fast casual chain Chipotle announced Monday that it is opening its first location in Mexico in the Monterrey area.

items.title: Warburg, Bain, CVC among PE firms in talks to buy into Agappe Diagnostics
items.summary: Several global private equity firms are exploring a significant stake purchase in Agappe Diagnostics. This potential deal involves both existing investor sales and new capital infusion. The transaction is expected to value the Kochi-based in-vitro diagnostics firm at over three thousand crore rupees. Agappe Diagnostics develops and manufactures diagnostic reagents and equipment for global markets.

items.title: Treasury yields move higher as U.S.-Iran ceasefire strained; investors await inflation data
items.summary: Treasury yields were broadly flat on Monday after the ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran came under increasing strain over the weekend.

items.title: NCLT refuses to reinstate Juliet Apparels founder Gunvant Trevadia as Director
items.summary: Mumbai-based Juliet Apparels Pvt. Ltd. owns the women's innerwear brand 'Juliet'. The dispute is between two branches of the family that founded the company.

items.title: Retail Inflation up to 4.4% on costlier food, transport
items.summary: India's retail inflation reached 4.4% in June, surpassing the central bank's target. Higher food and transport costs drove this increase, marking the highest print under the new CPI series. Food inflation accelerated to 5.3%, crossing the five percent mark for the first time. Core inflation moderated to 3.9%, partly due to falling gold and silver prices. Economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will maintain its current policy rate.

items.title: India's ethanol surplus spurs export push amid E20 backlash
items.summary: Industry participants said the country is considering exporting ethanol to neighbouring countries, including Nepal, Bangladesh, and Indonesia, which have 10% ethanol blending targets but lack adequate feedstock and distilling capacity.

items.title: Seven Indian-flagged ships operating as per schedule in Persian Gulf
items.summary: Seven Indian flagged vessels are operating safely in the Persian Gulf as per schedule. These ships are not carrying oil, gas, or fertilizers destined for India. About 148 seafarers are onboard these Indian flagged vessels with supplies provided. One Indian seafarer was missing after an attack on a Cyprus flagged ship. Vessels are exercising caution and alternative routes are becoming the norm.

items.title: India-UK CETA duty benefits to require CBIC digital authentication
items.summary: India will implement digital authentication for UK exporter origin declarations starting July 15. This new process requires customs to issue a Unique Reference Number before duty benefits are granted. UK exporters must email declarations to designated Indian customs email addresses for verification. Indian customs will authenticate data shared by UK authorities for this purpose. This system aims to prevent fraudulent claims and ensure compliance with trade agreement rules.

items.title: Renewable energy developers get one-time exit for unused grid connectivity
items.summary: The power sector regulator has introduced a new framework for renewable energy projects. Developers can now surrender, transfer, or retain unused interstate transmission connectivity. This move aims to free up grid capacity for projects ready for commissioning. The regulator estimates 15.7 GW of connectivity could be released through this initiative. Developers retaining connectivity without a PPA must furnish a bank guarantee and commission projects within 24 months.

items.title: India-EU trade deal legal review nearing completion: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the European Union are nearing the end of their trade pact's legal review. This agreement is expected to become operational a few months after the India-UK deal. India's commerce minister noted strong alignment among EU member states on this partnership. He also highlighted significant potential to increase India-Spain bilateral trade and tourism. Both nations aim to boost investments and collaborations in education and talent development.

items.title: US framework trade deal ready, to be inked at the right time: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal
items.summary: India and the US are progressing well on a proposed trade agreement. The framework deal is ready and will be signed at the right time. Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a balanced and commercially meaningful agreement. Negotiations are continuing constructively, addressing all aspects of the trade relationship. The trade deal will include pathways for resolving ongoing investigations and preventing new tariffs.

items.title: Nirmala Sitharaman calls for greater NRI outreach to boost foreign currency inflows
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged banks to intensify outreach to non-resident Indians. Innovative deposit products and foreign currency inflows are key objectives for banks. These initiatives aim to bolster forex reserves and support the Indian rupee. Banks reported significant interest from non-resident Indians across various jurisdictions. The Reserve Bank of India is actively supporting banks in deposit mobilization efforts.

items.title: Waller says Fed shouldn't 'fight the last war' on inflation but warns hikes still possible
items.summary: The Fed governor said inflation has expanded beyond the often-cited drivers such as the energy price spike in tariffs.

items.title: Wage, fuel spike hit ATM cash services in four states
items.summary: The one-day disruption affected ATM uptime and cash availability across the four states, with intermittent service gaps reported as regular cash replenishment cycles were interrupted. Industry executives, however, said operations are expected to resume from Tuesday after several private sector banks agreed to renegotiate contracts and revise service fees, while discussions with other lenders continue.

items.title: Paramount, WBD hit with lawsuit from 12 states, including California, to block merger
items.summary: A group of states, including California, filed a lawsuit to block the merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: Emcure buys remaining 12.05% in Gennova to take full control
items.summary: Emcure Pharma is acquiring the remaining stake in Gennova Biopharmaceuticals. This move simplifies ownership and sharpens strategic focus for the subsidiary. Gennova will now be led by Samit Mehta for its next growth phase. The company will continue leveraging its biomanufacturing platforms for new products. Gennova's former CEO Sanjay Singh has divested its mRNA business.

items.title: Price hikes keep budget phone, TV buyers away
items.summary: Entry-level smartphone and television sales have declined for the first time in over a decade. Successive price increases, driven by memory chip costs, are deterring first-time buyers. Mid-range and premium device demand continues to grow at a faster pace than last year. This shift has prompted companies to reduce their model portfolios across categories. Some manufacturers are re-entering the non-smart TV market to offer lower prices.

items.title: RBI Deputy Guv pushes for meaningful, fair grievance redressal
items.summary: The conference brought together IOs from across a representative spectrum of regulated entities including banks, non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), credit information companies (CICs) and other regulated entities, along with MD, EDs and CEOs charge of customer service, as well as RBI Ombudsmen and senior officials of the Reserve Bank.

items.title: Gen Z in 'high spirits' as boomers wind down
items.summary: Younger Indians aged 21-28 are drinking alcohol more frequently than before. This trend challenges the idea that Gen Z avoids alcoholic beverages. Older generations are reducing consumption while younger consumers explore premium options. Urban affluent Indians show increased alcohol participation, favoring bars and restaurants. Whisky remains the most consumed spirit, with premium brands gaining popularity.

items.title: Russia to bring flagship INNOPROM industrial fair to India in September
items.summary: Russia's INNOPROM industrial trade fair will debut in India from September 9 to 11. This event aims to boost industrial cooperation and bilateral trade between the two nations. Key sectors for collaboration include pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and advanced manufacturing. Discussions are also underway for a bilateral investment protection treaty. India and Russia anticipate achieving $70 billion in bilateral trade this financial year.

items.title: Maharashtra withdraws 2023 circular on minor mineral levy during land development
items.summary: Maharashtra government withdrew a May 2023 circular on minor mineral royalty. This move provides relief to developers facing legal disputes over the framework. The earlier policy faced challenges for inconsistency with court judgments. A fresh, transparent framework will be framed after considering judicial directions. Until then, a new procedure for excavation permissions will be issued.

items.title: Some ships still crossing Strait of Hormuz despite security threat
items.summary: A small number of vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, despite the heightened security threats as Tehran and Washington battle for control of the strategic waterway. Just 14 vessels -- half of which were commercial ships -- crossed on Sunday, according to data from maritime tracker Kpler, suggesting the waterway is not completely closed as Iran had announced.

Deregistration will eliminate lease rental costs for the grounded assets. SpiceJet stated there is no impact on current airline operations. The airline continues discussions with lessors and manufacturers for aircraft return.

items.summary: India and the European Union expect to finish their trade pact legal scrubbing soon. This agreement will become operational a few months after the India-UK trade deal. items.title: First vaccine for Bundibugyo Ebola hits human trials in Oxford study
items.summary: Oxford's Ebola vaccine candidate has entered Phase 1 clinical trials for safety evaluation. The ChAdOx1 BDBV vaccine uses a platform similar to the Covid-19 vaccine. Serum Institute of India manufactured approximately 620,000 doses for potential future use. This initiative builds on a strategic partnership with CEPI and the University of Oxford. Late-stage trials will follow if Phase 1 results prove successful.

items.summary: The 20-year concession agreements for two highway stretches spanning 170 km in Tamil Nadu expand the multi-strategy platform’s infrastructure portfolio to 26 assets across 17 states.

items.title: RBI deputy guv asks financial entities' ombudsmen to provide effective complaint resolution
items.summary: Reserve Bank Deputy Governor Swaminathan J urged internal ombudsmen to ensure fair complaint resolutions. He asked them to identify recurring issues and implement remedial measures effectively. An empowered internal ombudsman mechanism reduces external escalations significantly. The conference focused on faster, higher-quality resolutions and systemic improvements. Participants exchanged views on enhancing the internal ombudsman framework's effectiveness.

items.title: Procedure for TRQs allocation under India-Oman trade pact notified
items.summary: India has notified procedures for tariff concessions under its free trade agreement with Oman. This agreement, which came into effect on June 1, offers reduced import duties on specific Omani goods. Items like dates and marble will benefit from these quota-based tariff reductions. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade issued the public notice regarding these tariff rate quotas.

items.title: Gold, silver prices today: Comex gold, silver extend losses as Middle East tensions lift Fed rate hike fears
items.summary: Gold and silver prices continued to fall amid Middle East tensions, inflation concerns, and a stronger US dollar. Gold futures dropped to $4,009 and silver to $57.82. Crude oil prices surged over 4%, raising inflation fears and impacting markets ahead of economic data and Fed Chair testimony.

items.title: Air India gets DGCA nod for electronic technical logbooks use for B787 and 777 planes
items.summary: India's aviation regulator DGCA has approved electronic logbooks for Air India's Boeing aircraft. This digital system will replace most paper-based maintenance records for these planes. Real-time information sharing will improve coordination and speed up defect reporting.

items.title: 21% YoY rise in engineering exports in June shows sector's resilience amid global challenges: EEPC India Chairman Pankaj Chadha
items.summary: Engineering exports rose nearly twenty-one percent year-on-year in June. This growth reached eleven point four eight billion dollars, reflecting sector resilience. Cumulative exports for the first quarter also showed an eighteen percent increase. Free trade agreements and market diversification contributed to this strong performance.

items.title: US to charge 20% on Strait of Hormuz cargo, says Trump; reinstates naval blockade on ​Iran
items.summary: President Donald Trump announced the United States is reinstating a naval blockade on Iran. He stated the U.S. will be reimbursed twenty percent on all cargo shipped through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claimed it had closed the vital waterway following an unauthorized transit. U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged missile and drone attacks over the weekend. This escalation casts doubt on a recent interim agreement to reopen the strait.

items.summary: Floor price has been fixed as ₹378.50 a share, a discount of 7.9 per cent to current price

items.title: Small biz outstanding grows 13% in FY26, asset quality improves: Report
items.summary: Small business credit exposure grew by 13.4 percent to Rs 49.2 lakh crore by March 2026. Asset quality improved, with overdue balances now at four percent. Sole proprietorship concerns led this significant portfolio expansion. State-run banks showed accelerated enterprise term loan portfolio growth. Manufacturing sector received thirty-one percent of these small business loans.

items.title: HDFC Bank's total workforce witnesses reduction of 3,343 employees in FY26
items.summary: HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees during the last fiscal year. The bank's total balance sheet expanded by twelve percent to over forty-three trillion rupees. Employee attrition stood at twenty-three percent, while senior management roles saw an increase.

items.title: India's exports to West Asia region evened out in June: Commerce Secretary
items.summary: India's exports to West Asia grew 7.29 percent to USD 5 billion in June. Shipments to the UAE increased while those to Saudi Arabia saw a decline. Imports from the UAE and Oman registered positive growth during this period. Indian exporters are utilizing Omani ports to facilitate regional trade. The US-Iran conflict had previously impacted international shipping routes.

items.title: Area sown to kharif crops remains 16% on weak monsoon; paddy, pulses acreage down: Agriculture Ministry
items.summary: Kharif crop sowing is 16 percent lower this season. This delay is attributed to a weak monsoon and El Nino effects. Area under pulses and coarse cereals has significantly declined. Oilseeds and cotton coverage also shows a notable decrease. Sugarcane and jute acreage, however, have seen slight increases.

items.title: BSNL unaudited operational revenue up by 10% to ₹4,418 crore in Q1: Scindia
items.summary: State-run telecom giant BSNL has provisionally posted around a 10% increase in revenue from operations in the first quarter of FY27, Union Telecom Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said today.

items.title: Aditya Birla Group acquires Shell's India renewables energy arm, Sprng Energy, for $1.8bn
items.summary: Aditya Birla Group announced acquiring Sprng Energy for $1.8 billion. This significant deal expands the group's renewable energy portfolio substantially. The acquisition includes utility-scale solar, wind, and hybrid assets across India. This transaction positions Aditya Birla Renewables among the nation's largest developers. Regulatory approvals are now pending for the completion of this major acquisition.

items.title: Mylan launches block deal to sell minority stake in Biocon for up to $363 million
items.summary: The Viatris arm looks to offload 92 million equity shares, marking a financial exit following a series of biosimilar asset integrations.

items.title: India's exports to US dip 1.21% in June, imports rise 33.86%
items.summary: India's exports to the US saw a slight dip in June, while imports increased significantly. Merchandise exports declined marginally during April-June, with imports showing a substantial rise. Trade with China experienced robust growth in both exports and imports during the period. Exports to Singapore and several other nations registered positive growth.

items.title: Laser Power & Infra IPO: Issue booked 41 times on Day 03; NII, QIB portions see strong demand
items.summary: Laser Power & Infra Ltd.'s IPO was oversubscribed 41 times, with institutional investors leading. The issue raised ₹742 crore, priced at ₹203-214 per share. The grey market premium suggests potential listing above the issue price, slated for July 16 after allotments on July 14.

items.title: India-UK trade pact: CBIC issues guidelines on implementation of self-certification of origin declarations
items.summary: New guidelines for self-certification of origin declarations are now in effect. This change applies to the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement starting July fifteenth. The agreement replaces traditional certificates with a self-declaration framework for origin verification. Customs authorities in both nations will manage the authentication process for imports. Origin declarations will remain valid for twelve months from their completion date.

items.title: Truecaller row: TRAI seeks 'designated agency' status under IT Act; Meity examining it
items.summary: Telecom regulator TRAI wants to be an authorized agency under the IT Act. This status would allow TRAI to take action against call management applications. The IT Ministry is currently examining this request for regulatory jurisdiction. TRAI recently asserted that no app can block government and regulated entity calls. The Ministry is reviewing the matter, not specifically the Truecaller dispute.

items.title: UP to get road projects worth Rs 5 lakh crore in next two years: RTH Minister Nitin Gadkari
items.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari announced road projects worth Rs 5 lakh crore for Uttar Pradesh. New projects worth Rs 50,000-60,000 crore have also been approved for the state. Infrastructure development is considered the foundation for economic growth and investment attraction. The minister highlighted the accelerated development pace in Uttar Pradesh since 2014.

items.title: IndiGo, Accor announce reciprocal loyalty programmes partnership
items.summary: IndiGo and Accor have announced a new loyalty partnership in India. Members can now convert reward points between IndiGo BluChip and ALL Accor. This collaboration connects air travel with rewarding hospitality experiences for customers. Travellers can seamlessly link flights and hotel stays across both networks. The partnership aims to create a more rewarding travel journey within India and beyond.
